spec_match.rationale: Markup uses `focusgroup="horizontal"` exactly as the Open UI explainer specifies and the page declares 'no JavaScript focus management' — but only `horizontal` is exercised. The explainer's other axis values (`vertical`, `block`, `inline`), and the `wrap` / `extend` / `no-memory` modifiers, are documented in the code-card but not in the live UI.

interactive.rationale: Real focusable rich-text-editor toolbar with role=toolbar, aria-pressed buttons, separators, and a contenteditable target — meets the bar even without JS focus management.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb promises Tab enters, arrows move, Tab exits; the page delivers exactly that markup pattern with separators and a downstream editor to Tab into.

edge_cases.rationale: Separators correctly use role=separator so arrow nav skips them, but no demo of: a disabled button (should be skipped), a non-button child (should not be focusable in the group), RTL behaviour (would need `block inline` not `horizontal`), or what happens in a browser that doesn't yet support the attribute (note says 'Chrome 150+' but no detection or graceful fallback message).

references.rationale: See-also links to https://chromestatus.com/feature/5637601087193088, the Open UI explainer, and the Edge demos.

design_system.rationale: Variables-driven, toolbar buttons use the global button style with pressed-state styling via aria-pressed, no static-card.

openQuestions.title: Add a feature-detection fallback message
openQuestions.detail: Page note says 'Chrome 150+ — requires focusgroup attribute'. Add a small inline detection (`'focusgroup' in HTMLElement.prototype` or similar) that flips a banner when the attribute is honoured vs ignored, so visitors on older Chrome see why the arrows don't work.

openQuestions.title: Demo a disabled button being skipped
openQuestions.detail: Real toolbars have disabled buttons (e.g. Bold inside a code block). The spec requires focusgroup arrow navigation to skip them. Add one `<button disabled>` and call out the expected behaviour so visitors learn this edge case.

openQuestions.title: Note the Chrome milestone mismatch
openQuestions.detail: The page lives in `v146/` but its support note says 'Chrome 150+'. The chromestatus entry is the v146 record so the page should explain what is changing in 146 specifically (origin trial? prefixed? behind a flag?). Right now the relationship is confusing.

summary: Honest, clean toolbar demo with proper ARIA — but only exercises `horizontal` and leaves the rich modifier vocabulary in a code-card. Add feature detection, a disabled-button edge case, and resolve the 146-vs-150 milestone confusion.
